objective: Close red-team gaps in auto thread-on-invoke routing, task continuation, and operator diagnostics
success_criteria: Missing stored response threads fail open to the current channel instead of dropping the invoke.
Parent-channel replies to a threaded source message resume the existing waiting or running task instead of forking a fresh turn.
Invoke-thread governance status surfaces missing bot thread permissions and enabling a broken channel policy is rejected.
constraints: Keep the fix surgical within the new auto thread-on-invoke routing, task matching, and governance status seams.
Preserve the explicit originChannelId/responseChannelId routing model introduced by the thread-on-invoke rewrite.

in: Threaded response-surface recovery in the Discord message-create handler
Task-run matching rules for waiting and active tasks replying from parent channels
Governance health and validation for invoke-thread channel policies
Regression coverage and changelog updates for the above behavior
out: Broad Discord tool-surface refactors unrelated to invoke-thread routing
Database schema changes beyond the already-added invoke-thread policy model

milestones.title: Capture intake snapshot and touched scope
notes: Work item scaffolded from the local workflow generator.

milestones.title: Implement the scoped changes
notes: Added fail-open recovery for missing stored response channels in messageCreate and in the background task worker.
Matched threaded parent-message replies back to waiting/running task runs by sourceMessageId only when the response surface is a distinct thread, and stopped reusing approval-paused threads.
Added invoke-thread permission health reporting and blocked enablement when Sage lacks required thread permissions.

milestones.title: Run validation and publish the completion report
notes: Ran workflow validation, lint, full test suite, build, docs checks, repeated trust runs, tool audit, and mutation-critical coverage.

Added targeted regressions for stale-surface fallback in both foreground and worker paths, threaded parent-message continuation, invoke-thread router status filtering, and governance permission diagnostics.
Final red-team pass found no new in-scope issues.

progress.summary: Created the local work item scaffold.
Closed the remaining worker and waiting-approval routing gaps, reran the full validation stack, and completed a clean final red-team pass.

decision_log.decision: Treat missing stored response surfaces as a recoverable routing failure and fall back to the current channel.
decision_log.rationale: The feature contract promises fail-open behavior; dropping the turn was a regression.

decision_log.decision: Match parent-message replies by sourceMessageId only for task runs whose response surface is a distinct thread.
decision_log.rationale: This preserves the new threaded continuation model without reintroducing same-channel auto-resume ambiguity.

decision_log.decision: Reject invoke-thread policy enablement when Sage currently lacks CreatePublicThreads or SendMessagesInThreads, and expose the same health in status reads.
decision_log.rationale: Operators should not silently enable a broken policy.

decision_log.decision: Treat worker fallback to the origin channel as an intentional response-surface reset and skip stale canonical-response revalidation for that publish attempt.
decision_log.rationale: Without that reset, the worker reloaded dead-thread metadata and talked itself out of publishing the recovered reply.

decision_log.decision: Limit source-message thread reuse to running and waiting-user-input task runs only.
decision_log.rationale: Approval-paused tasks should not attract fresh parent-channel invokes into their old thread.

discoveries.summary: The stale completed-task reopen path reused persisted response-channel metadata before checking whether the underlying Discord thread still existed.
evidence: The red-team pass reproduced an early return in messageCreate when a persisted responseChannelId could not be rehydrated.

discoveries.summary: Task matching only recognized replies to Sage's response message; parent-channel replies to the original source message were routed into the old thread but still forked a fresh turn.
evidence: The auto-thread routing reused response threads by sourceMessageId, but the waiting/running repo matchers only checked responseMessageId until this hardening pass.

discoveries.summary: The background worker still reloaded stale canonical-response metadata after falling back from a deleted thread, which prevented the recovered reply from being published.
evidence: The new worker regression initially failed because refreshWorkerResponseSessionState repopulated the dead thread's responseMessageId after the fallback path had already cleared the in-memory response-session state.

validation_strategy: Run targeted Vitest coverage for agentTaskRunRepo, messageCreate routing, and governance wrappers before the full suite.
Run workflow validation plus lint, full tests, build, docs gates, and trust deep because the changes touch runtime code and tracked Markdown.
notes: The deep trust gate briefly created a local mutation report under reports/; the generated file was removed so the workspace stays aligned to the canonical artifact layout.
